questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should films be included in the Concurrent List?
Arguments: I. Yes. It will give respect to the views of the states.
II. No. It will deteriorate the standard of films.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should there be only one university throughout India?
Arguments: I. Yes. This is the only way to bring about uniformity in educational standards.
II. This is administratively impossible.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should mercy death be legalised?
Arguments: I. Yes. Patients undergoing terrible suffering and having absolutely no chance of recovery should be liberated from suffering through mercy death.
II. No. Even mercy death is a sort of killing and killing cannot be legalised.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should there be a single government in the world?
Arguments: I. Yes. It will help in eliminating tensions among the nations.
II. No. Because then, only the developed countries will dominate in the government.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should the institution of marriages be abolished?
Arguments: I. Yes. It is already showing cracks.
II. No. It is necessary for the survival of society.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should healthcare service be nationalised?
Arguments: I. Yes. It has been done elsewhere also.
II. No. The quality of healthcare service will deteriorate.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should telecasting feature films be stopped?
Arguments: I. Yes. Young children are misguided by feature films.
II. No. This is the only way to educate the masses.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should defence in rural India be mechanised?
Arguments: I. Yes. It would lead to higher production.
II. No. It would lead to rural unemployment.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should the illiterate be debarred from voting?
Arguments: I. Yes. They are easily misguided.
II. No. It is their constitutional right.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Should luxury hotels be banned in India?
Arguments: I. Yes. They serve from where international criminals operate.
II. No. Affluent foreign tourists will have no place to stay.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: Can pollution be controlled?
Arguments: I. Yes. If every one realises the hazard it may pose and cooperates to get rid of it, pollution may be controlled.
II. No. The crowded highways, factories and industries are an ever-growing population eager to acquire more and more land for constructing houses are beyond control.

choices.c: Either I or II is strong
choices.d: Neither I nor II is strong
choices.e: Both I and II are strong
